name: shs-tools

metadata.name: shs-tools
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)
metadata.summary: An assortment of little helper functions

documentation:
# shs-tools

An assortment of helper functions, primarily developed for use in [Advent Of Code](https://adventofcode.com/)

functions.name: tools.tools.get_script_dir
documentation: return path of the executed script

functions.name: tools.tools.compare
documentation: compare to values, return -1 if a is smaller than b, 1 if a is greater than b, 0 is both are equal

functions.name: tools.tools.minmax
documentation: return the min and max value of an array (or arbitrary amount of arguments)

functions.name: tools.int_seq.factorial
documentation:
n! = 1 * 2 * 3 * 4 * ... * n
1, 1, 2, 6, 24, 120, 720, ...

If you're looking for efficiency with start == 1, just use math.factorial(n)
which takes just 25% of the compute time on average, but this is the fastest
pure python implementation I could come up with and it allows for partial
multiplications, like 5 * 6 * 7 * 8 * .... * 17

functions.name: tools.int_seq.pentagonal
documentation:
A pentagonal number is a figurate number that extends the concept of triangular and square numbers to the pentagon
0, 1, 5, 12, 22, 35, ...

methods.name: tools.coordinate.Coordinate.getDistanceTo
documentation:
Get distance to target Coordinate

```
param target



param algorithm

Calculation Algorithm (s. DistanceAlgorithm)

param includeDiagonals

in Manhattan Mode specify if diagonal
movements are allowed (counts as 1.4 in 2D, 1.7 in 3D)

return

Distance to Target
```

methods.name: tools.coordinate.Coordinate.getNeighbours
documentation:
Get a list of neighbouring coordinates.

```
param includeDiagonal

include diagonal neighbours

param minX

ignore all neighbours that would have an X value below this

param minY

ignore all neighbours that would have an Y value below this

param minZ

ignore all neighbours that would have an Z value below this

param maxX

ignore all neighbours that would have an X value above this

param maxY

ignore all neighbours that would have an Y value above this

param maxZ

ignore all neighbours that would have an Z value above this

param dist

distance to neighbour coordinates

return

list of Coordinate
```

methods.name: tools.coordinate.Coordinate.getAngleTo
documentation: normalized returns an angle going clockwise with 0 starting in the 'north'

methods.name: tools.coordinate.Coordinate.getLineTo
documentation: this will probably not yield what you expect, when using float coordinates

methods.name: tools.coordinate.Shape.__init__
documentation:
in 2D mode: top_left is the upper left corner and bottom_right the lower right
            (top_left.x <= bottom_right.x and top_left.y <= bottom_right.y)
in 3D mode: same logic applied, just for 3D Coordinates
            top_left is the upper left rear corner and bottom_right the lower right front
            (top_left.x <= bottom_right.x and top_left.y <= bottom_right.y and top_left.z <= bottom_right.z)

methods.name: tools.coordinate.Shape.intersection
documentation: returns a Shape of the intersecting part, or None if the Shapes don't intersect

classes.name: tools.coordinate.Line
documentation: FIXME: Line could probably also just be a subclass of Shape

methods.name: tools.coordinate.Polygon.__init__
documentation: points have to be in (counter)clockwise order, not repeating the first coordinate

functions.name: tools.itertools.len_combinations
documentation: How many options will itertools.combinations(iterable, r) yield?

functions.name: tools.itertools.len_permutations
documentation: How many options will itertools.permutations(iterable, r) yield?

functions.name: tools.itertools.len_combinations_of_sum
documentation:
How many options will combinations_of_sum(total_sum, length) yield?

No idea how to factor in min_value, yet, so if using min_value, the answer will always be too high

functions.name: tools.aoc_ocr.convert_6

functions.name: tools.aoc_ocr.convert_array_6

functions.name: tools.aoc_ocr._convert_6
documentation: Convert a prepared height 6 array to characters

methods.name: tools.simplesocket.Socket.recvline
documentation:
> Receive exactly one text line (delimiter: newline "

" or "
") from the socket.

> ```
> param timeout
>
> wait at most TIMEOUT seconds for a newline to appear in the buffer
>
> return
>
> Either a str containing a line received or None if no newline was found
> ```

modules.name: tools.daemon
documentation: Shamelessly stolen from https://gist.github.com/josephernest/77fdb0012b72ebdf4c9d19d6256a1119
From "A simple unix/linux daemon in Python" by Sander Marechal
See http://stackoverflow.com/a/473702/1422096 and
http://web.archive.org/web/20131017130434/http://www.jejik.com/articles/2007/02/a_simple_unix_linux_daemon_in_python/
Modified to add quit() that allows to run some code before closing the daemon
See http://stackoverflow.com/a/40423758/1422096
Modified for Python 3
(see also: http://web.archive.org/web/20131017130434/http://www.jejik.com/files/examples/daemon3x.py)
Joseph Ernest, 20200507_1220

classes.name: tools.daemon.Daemon

methods.name: tools.daemon.Daemon.daemonize
documentation:
do the UNIX double-fork magic, see Stevens' "Advanced
Programming in the UNIX Environment" for details (ISBN 0201563177)
http://www.erlenstar.demon.co.uk/unix/faq_2.html#SEC16

methods.name: tools.daemon.Daemon.start
documentation: Start the daemon

methods.name: tools.daemon.Daemon.stop
documentation: Stop the daemon

methods.name: tools.daemon.Daemon.restart
documentation: Restart the daemon

methods.name: tools.daemon.Daemon.run
documentation:
You should override this method when you subclass Daemon. It will be called after the process has been
daemonized by start() or restart().

methods.name: tools.daemon.Daemon.quit
documentation: You should override this method when you subclass Daemon. It will be called before the process is stopped.

A generic daemon class.

Usage: subclass the Daemon class and override the run() method

functions.name: tools.math.round_half_up
documentation: pythons round() rounds .5 to the *even* number; 0.5 == 0

functions.name: tools.math.mul
documentation: similar to sum(), just for multiplication

methods.name: tools.grid.Grid.from_data
documentation:
Every entry in data will be treated as row, every entry in data[entry] will be a separate column.
gen_3d = True will just add z=0 to every Coordinate
translate is used on every data[entry] and if present as key, its value will be used instead
    a value in translate can be a function with the following signature: def translate(value: Any) -> Any
    a key in translate is either a string of len 1 or it will be treated as regexp
        if multiple regexp match, the first encountered wins
        if there is a key that matches the entry it wins over any mathing regexp

methods.name: tools.aoc.AOCDay.getMultiLineInputAsArray
documentation: get input for day x as 2d array, split by empty lines

methods.name: tools.aoc.AOCDay.getInputAsArraySplit

class_variables.name: tools.aoc.AOCDay.input
